title: Orchestrator Force-Unclaim for Unresponsive Agents

story.i_want_to: force-unclaim a story from an unresponsive agent
story.so_that: stuck stories can be reassigned without manual database intervention

rationale: When an implementation agent crashes mid-work, the story remains in 'assigned' or 'implementing' status with no way to reassign it. The assigned agent is the only one allowed to unclaim (US-7.1 AC-7.1.5). Without a force-unclaim mechanism, a single agent crash can block an entire dependency chain indefinitely. The orchestrator needs an override.

acceptance_criteria.description: POST /api/v1/stories/:id/force-unclaim resets agent_status to 'pending', clears assigned_agent_id, assigned_at. Requires orchestrator role (or superadmin via impersonation).
Force-unclaim works regardless of current agent_status (assigned, implementing, or reported_done). It is a hard reset.
The force-unclaim is recorded in the audit log with action='force_unclaimed', including the orchestrator's identity and the previously assigned agent.
A webhook event story.force_unclaimed is emitted.
Force-unclaim on a story that is already pending returns 200 (idempotent), not an error.
Force-unclaim does NOT reset verified_status. A verified story can be force-unclaimed (returning it to pending agent_status while retaining its verification).
Agent role keys CANNOT call force-unclaim (returns 403). Only orchestrator and superadmin.
Tenant isolation: force-unclaim only works on stories within the authenticated tenant.

test_cases.name: Orchestrator force-unclaims an implementing story
preconditions: story = fixture(:story, tenant: tenant, agent_status: :implementing, assigned_agent_id: agent.id)

test_cases.name: Force-unclaim on pending story is idempotent

technical_notes: Context function: Progress.force_unclaim_story(tenant_id, story_id, orchestrator_agent_id, opts)
Use Ecto.Multi: update story + audit log + webhook event in same transaction
All context functions MUST return {:ok, result} or {:error, reason} tuples

Testing infrastructure: (1) All tests use async: true via DataCase/ConnCase, (2) Mox mocks defined in test/support/mocks.ex, (3) Default permissive stubs set in DataCase/ConnCase setup via stub_all_defaults/0 and Mox.set_mox_from_context(tags) for async isolation, (4) setup :verify_on_exit! in every test module, (5) Fixtures in test/support/fixtures.ex with build/2 (no DB) and fixture/2 (DB insert with auto-dependency resolution), (6) NEVER Application.put_env in tests — all service swapping via config/test.exs.
Config-based DI: behaviours resolved via Application.get_env(:loopctl, :key, DefaultModule). config/test.exs maps behaviours to Mox mocks. Tests use expect/3 to set specific expectations.
